MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-07-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY CASE PENDING ARBITRATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT GENTRY IS NOT A PARTY TO THE SALES REPRESENTATIVE AGREEMENT AND LACKS STANDING TO SEEK ARBITRATION. PER 1281.2(C) THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS. IT MAKES LITTLE SENSE TO ADJUDICATE CLAIMS FOR INDEMNITY BEFORE THE UNDERLYING TORT CLAIMS ARE RESOLVED. DEFENDANT GENTRY'S UNTIMELY ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE APPLICATION OF ILLINOIS LAW WERE NOT CONSIDERED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA CSR # 5153
ORDER GRANTING APPLICATION TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR ATTORNEY NAT SHAPO FILED BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST SIX CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THE EXISTENCE OF A TOLLING AGREEMENT OR OTHERWISE TO ALLEGE WHY THE FIRST SIX CLAIMS ARE NOT TIME-BARRED AND TO CLARIFY THAT THE CONTRACT IS ORAL. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS NEITHER NECESSARY NOR PROPER AND THUS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1061. DEFENDANT'S REMAINING ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM PRIVILEGE WAIVER AND ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S OWN AUTHORITY TO REVISIT ITS OWN RULINGS UNDER THE INTEREST OF JUSTICE UNDER THE LE FRANCOIS OPINION OF THE CA SUPREME COURT. GENTRY IS RELIEVED FROM ITS WAIVER OF OBJECTIONS BASED UPON THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE. SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,500.00 IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S MOTION For Relief From Privilege Waiver And Order Compelling Production Of Privileged Documents IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S OWN AUTHORITY TO REVISIT ITS OWN RULINGS UNDER THE INTEREST OF JUSTICE UNDER THE LE FRANCOIS OPINION OF THE CA SUPREME COURT. GENTRY IS RELIEVED FROM ITS WAIVER OF OBJECTIONS BASED UPON THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE. SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,500.00 IS GRANTED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case Pending Arbitration is Denied. Defendant Gentry is not a party to the Sales Representative Agreement and lacks standing to seek arbitration. Per 1281.2(c) there is a substantial risk of inconsistent rulings. It makes little sense to adjudicate claims for indemnity before the underlying tort claims are resolved. Defendant Gentry's untimely arguments regarding the application of Illinois law were not considered. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first six causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege the existence of a tolling agreement or otherwise to allege why the first six claims are not time-barred and to clarify that the contract is oral. The seventh cause of action for declaratory relief is neither necessary nor proper and thus is sustained without leave to amend per Code of Civil Procedure section 1061. Defendant's remaining arguments lack merit. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)
